codeReview.summary: The feature successfully exposed semantic identity/link fields on parser summaries, but the new provenance contract is still incomplete because the block/layout regression only exercises parser-facing tuples and never proves that outward summaries themselves distinguish layout and block records. A blocking gap remains against the feature's requirement that outward summaries carry the semantic identity fields needed to distinguish same-kind block/layout records.

issues.description: The new block/layout regression validates `pending_provenance_projection()` instead of the actual outward summary surface (`summarize_object()` / `ParsedRecordSummary`). Because this helper re-labels objects with hard-coded `target` strings and only copies summary fields afterwards, the feature still lacks coverage that outward summaries themselves distinguish layout vs block records on parser-facing output, which is required by the feature's expected behavior.

summary: Reviewed the feature metadata, worker handoff/transcript, commit `1b75be5`, and the relevant parser files. Found one blocking issue: the added block/layout test stops at a custom provenance projection helper instead of asserting the outward summary seam directly, so the feature does not yet prove that parser-facing summaries distinguish block/layout identities as required.
